qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cross-thread environment variable contamination via PHP putenv() in FrankenPHP multi-threaded runtime
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets env.go go_putenv() calling os.Setenv() and leaking process-wide environment variables, not the advisory's missing deletion of $_SESSION in frankenphp_reset_super_globals.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described FrankenPHP worker-mode $_SESSION leak, where $_SESSION remains in EG(symbol_table) because frankenphp_reset_super_globals failed to delete it between worker requests.
evidence: The advisory root cause is cross-request session data leakage before session_start() due to missing $_SESSION cleanup in FrankenPHP worker-mode superglobal reset logic.
putenv_cross_thread_env_contamination discusses env.go go_putenv(), os.Setenv(), and process-wide environment variable contamination across requests and child processes.
